1. [phase-2-bff-clients] Phase 2 — BFF client layer: typed HttpClients in Web (IRegistrationApi, IBirthDeclarationApi, IChangeOfAddressApi) with officer forwarding

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Starting implementation of the Phase 2 BFF client layer with typed HttpClients in Web.

Mapping out the specific tasks for Phase 2. Will add typed HttpClients under `Web/Api/` for registration, birth declarations, and change of address APIs. Reusing Application DTOs as client contracts. Adding an `OfficerForwardingHandler` delegating handler and an `ApiException` helper to map HTTP validation errors.

HttpClient registration will use Aspire service discovery URLs. The exit criteria require all three client interfaces to be implemented and registered, successful end-to-end API calls with demo officer headers, and validation of document upload and download paths over HTTP. Now exploring the codebase structure.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The implementation mapping is now complete.

The implementation plan covers five components. A shared header constants file will live in Web/Api without referencing the Api project. An OfficerForwardingHandler will act as a delegating handler. A base class will handle common HTTP operations and error handling. An ApiException will map ProblemDetails responses.

Three typed HttpClient interfaces and their implementations are next. Dependency injection registration and Program.cs updates are required. An end-to-end proof test will verify client calls to the API with demo officer headers.

Integration tests will live in Integration.Tests. The exit criteria include validating document upload and download paths over HTTP. The HttpClient base address must use the Aspire service discovery URL format.

The DemoOfficerHeaders constants will move to the Application project rather than duplicating them in Web. This keeps the Web project free of Api references while still allowing both the Api middleware and the Web forwarding handler to share the same header definitions.

Moving DemoOfficerHeaders to the Application project is out of scope for Phase 2. The constants will instead be duplicated in Web/Api to avoid scope creep. The Aspire HttpClient registration pattern uses https+http://api as the base address.

The Aspire service discovery base address will be configured via `AddServiceDefaults()` and the `https+http://` scheme. The base client class will be declared as an internal abstract class accepting an injected `HttpClient`.

The base client class will expose generic GetAsync, PostAsync, PutAsync, and DeleteAsync methods. These helpers will handle typed serialization and cancellation tokens for all HTTP operations.

The base client class will also support multipart POST uploads, stream downloads, and string GET responses. A private helper will validate HTTP responses and throw ApiException when errors occur. ApiException will expose StatusCode and Title properties.

The `ThrowIfErrorAsync` helper will map specific HTTP status codes to domain exceptions. Validation problems become `FluentValidation.ValidationException`. Unauthorized requests become `UnauthorizedAccessException`. Missing resources become `KeyNotFoundException`. Conflicts become `InvalidOperationException`. Other errors fall back to `ApiException`. The developer is checking how Blazor catches these validation exceptions.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Fully qualified type aliases or namespace imports will be used for the interfaces to avoid ambiguity.

A shared base client class will support roughly seventy methods across the three API clients. DI registration will be added via `AddMunicipalApiClients` in `Web/Api/DependencyInjection.cs`. Unit tests will use `MunicipalAppFactory` in `BffApiClientTests.cs`.

The tests will configure HttpClient with `OfficerForwardingHandler` and verify `ListCases`, `OpenCase`, `AttachDocument`, and `DownloadDocument`. A scoped `ICurrentOfficer` mock is needed for the handler. The test class will implement `IClassFixture<MunicipalAppFactory>` and hold an `IRegistrationApi` instance.

The test constructor registers `ICurrentOfficer` as scoped and configures `IRegistrationApi` with `OfficerForwardingHandler` through `AddHttpClient`, then resolves the service from the built provider.

The test setup will resolve `IRegistrationApi` directly from the service provider after configuring `AddHttpClient`, since typed HttpClient factories do not support `GetRequiredService` on the interface alone. The `CurrentOfficer` default is a PopulationOfficer named Marie, so the test fixture must explicitly set the current officer before each request.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Starting implementation of the BFF client layer files.

Planning the specific files to create: demo header helpers, an officer forwarding handler, API exception classes, a base client, dependency injection extensions, typed clients for registration, birth declaration, and change of address, plus updates to Program.cs.

Also planning integration tests for the BFF client layer. The base client will support JSON serialization, multipart uploads, stream downloads, HTML responses, and various HTTP methods including empty POST bodies.

Adding a URI query builder helper that escapes key-value pairs. ApiException mapping will read ValidationProblemDetails to extract structured error details.

Mapping HTTP 403 to UnauthorizedAccessException and 404 to KeyNotFoundException. Reviewing which domain exceptions Blazor catches for 409 conflicts, such as InvalidRegistrationTransitionException and NationalRegisterConflictException.

Phase 2 will prioritize mapping ValidationProblemDetails to ValidationException so existing Blazor catch blocks remain compatible. Other HTTP errors will use ApiException with status codes. For 409 conflicts, InvalidOperationException may be thrown to match catch blocks that BirthDeclaration already handles.

Phase 2 will focus on validation mapping and client functionality. Full exception mapping is deferred to Phase 3. HTTP 400 maps to ValidationException, 403 to UnauthorizedAccessException, 404 to KeyNotFoundException, 409 to InvalidOperationException, and other errors to ApiException. Starting with RegistrationApiClient, the largest client.

Internal base class methods will be used for shared client logic. Optional query parameters are planned for SearchNationalRegisterRequest and ListOutboundNotifications. The IssueResidenceCertificate endpoint returns HTML content.
</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Starting implementation by creating infrastructure files first, then building out the three BFF client implementations.

Drafting the abstract `MunicipalApiClientBase` class with a generic `GetJsonAsync` helper for typed JSON responses.

Adding `GetStringAsync` and `PostJsonAsync` helpers to the base class.

Adding a generic `PostJsonAsync` overload to `MunicipalApiClientBase` that accepts a request payload and returns a typed response.

Adding `PutJsonAsync` and `DeleteJsonAsync` helpers to `MunicipalApiClientBase`.

Adding a `DownloadStreamAsync` helper to `MunicipalApiClientBase` that returns a `Stream` for large file downloads.

Adding a `PostMultipartFileAsync` helper to `MunicipalApiClientBase` for multipart file uploads with typed JSON responses.

Adding a `BuildQuery` helper to `MunicipalApiClientBase` for building URL query strings with escaped parameters.

Noting that `SearchNationalRegister` birth dates must be formatted as ISO dates.

The `PostMultipartFileAsync` stream must remain open until the request completes; callers should manage stream lifecycle.

Refining `DownloadStreamAsync` to avoid a thimmon pattern issue. The method will return the response stream directly so the caller can dispose the underlying HTTP response when the stream closes.

Copying the response stream into a `MemoryStream` avoids the thimmon pattern issue. The original HTTP response is disposed after the copy. This is acceptable for municipal document sizes.

Proceeding to write all implementation files in batches.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Phase 2 is complete. The Web project now has typed HTTP clients that call the Api with demo officer forwarding.

## What was added

**Infrastructure** (`Web/Api/`):
- `OfficerForwardingHandler` — forwards `X-Demo-Officer-*` headers from `ICurrentOfficer`
- `ApiException` — maps HTTP errors to familiar exceptions (`ValidationException` for 400, `UnauthorizedAccessException` for 403, `KeyNotFoundException` for 404, `InvalidOperationException` for 409)
- `MunicipalApiClientBase` — shared JSON, multipart upload, and stream download helpers
- `DependencyInjection.AddMunicipalApiClients()` — registers all three clients with Aspire service discovery (`https+http://api`)

**Typed clients** (all 70 endpoints covered):
- `IRegistrationApi` / `RegistrationApiClient` — 38 methods under `/api/registration/*`
- `IBirthDeclarationApi` / `BirthDeclarationApiClient` — 17 methods under `/api/birth-declarations/*`
- `IChangeOfAddressApi` / `ChangeOfAddressApiClient` — 15 methods under `/api/change-of-address/*`

All clients reuse Application DTOs as contracts — no duplicate type definitions.

**Registration** in `Program.cs`:
```csharp
builder.Services.AddMunicipalApiClients();
```

## Tests

Four integration tests in `tests/.../Api/BffApiClientTests.cs` verify:
- End-to-end API calls with officer header forwarding
- Registration document upload/download over HTTP
- Birth declaration document upload/download over HTTP
- Validation error mapping to `ValidationException`

All 94 integration tests pass.

## Phase 3 readiness

Blazor pages still inject handlers directly — that's intentional for Phase 2. When you're ready for Phase 3, the migration pattern is:

```diff
- @inject GetRegistrationCaseHandler GetHandler
+ @inject IRegistrationApi RegistrationApi

- _case = await GetHandler.Handle(new GetRegistrationCaseRequest(CaseId), ct);
+ _case = await RegistrationApi.GetCaseAsync(CaseId, ct);
```
